topic: French and American oak flavour intensity

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Wine matures in barrels made from oak species and grain structures commonly associated with French or American sources.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Oak species, grain, seasoning and toast influence the amount and balance of lactones, vanillin, tannins and toast-derived compounds extracted into wine.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: American oak often gives more overt vanilla and coconut character, while French oak is often perceived as subtler and more restrained; cooperage and use can alter either pattern.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: oak origin can influence flavour intensity, but barrel construction, toast, age and contact also matter.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
